signals: La inversión en infraestructura de IA desplaza al consumo como motor principal de la industria de semiconductores.
Los sistemas de agentes autónomos requieren marcos de gobernanza deóntica para operar de forma segura en empresas.
Google DeepMind simplifica arquitecturas multimodales eliminando encoders en su nuevo modelo Gemma 4 12B.
Intel rompe silos de confidencialidad y se alía con PDF Solutions para resolver problemas de rendimiento en nodos.
La IA conversacional AMIE iguala a médicos de cabecera en el manejo y diagnóstico de enfermedades complejas.
risks: Complejidad crítica en la gobernanza y cumplimiento legal de agentes de IA con capacidad de ejecución autónoma.
Persistencia de fallos de rendimiento (yield) en procesos de fabricación de semiconductores de vanguardia.
Carencia de marcos de seguridad robustos para la coordinación de múltiples agentes a través de redes corporativas.
watch: Efectividad de la arquitectura sin encoder de Gemma 4 en tareas de visión y lenguaje unificadas.
Impacto real de la alianza Intel-PDF Solutions en la recuperación de cuota de mercado de fabricación.
Adopción de políticas de control en tiempo real para agentes que pueden manipular datos y software.

items.title: The Yield Partnership: Intel and PDF Solutions Tackle Advanced Nodes
items.summary: One of the most difficult things to do in life is ask for help. This is inherently a big problem in the semiconductor industry dating back to the IDM days where silos of secrecy were established. As a result Intel has struggled with yield since the 14nm FinFET process nodes. items.title: Deontic Policies for Runtime Governance of Agentic AI Systems
items.summary: arXiv:2606.19464v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous agentic AI systems driven by Large Language Models (LLMs) introduce a new class of security, privacy, and compliance challenges: an agent that can invoke tools, manipulate data, install software, and coordinate with peer agents across organizational boundaries must be constrained not just by authentication and access control, but by the full structure of enterprise governance. This includes specifying what agents are permitted and prohibited from doing, what they areobliged to do after certain actions (e.g., notify the CISO), under what conditions a standing obligation may be waived, and which rules take precedence when policies conflict. This governance problem exceeds what current policy engines provide. Systems such as XACML, Rego, and Cedar address only the permit/prohibit subset of this governance structure. They do not provide obligation lifecycle management, meta-policy conflict resolution, dispensations that waive obligations in specific circumstances, and ontological reasoning over domain class hierarchies commonly found in applications such as healthcare, cybersecurity, or data privacy. We propose AgenticRei, which realizes key governance requirements such as obligations, dispensations, policy conflict resolutions, and reasoning over policies, as well as the basic permit/prohibit constraints. We use a deontic policy language built on the Rei framework, expressed as OWL (Web Ontology Language) and evaluated at runtime by a high-performance logic engine entirely outside the LLM. The same pipeline governs both tool invocations by the agent and agent-to-agent messages. We show through examples that deontic policies capture governance constraints around security and privacy that mostly cannot be expressed in current production engines. Our approach composes naturally with industry-standard frameworks like A2AS.
items.raw_title: Deontic Policies for Runtime Governance of Agentic AI Systems
items.raw_summary: arXiv:2606.19464v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous agentic AI systems driven by Large Language Models (LLMs) introduce a new class of security, privacy, and compliance challenges: an agent that can invoke tools, manipulate data, install software, and coordinate with peer agents across organizational boundaries must be constrained not just by authentication and access control, but by the full structure of enterprise governance. This includes specifying what agents are permitted and prohibited from doing, what they areobliged to do after certain actions (e.g., notify the CISO), under what conditions a standing obligation may be waived, and which rules take precedence when policies conflict. This governance problem exceeds what current policy engines provide. Systems such as XACML, Rego, and Cedar address only the permit/prohibit subset of this governance structure. They do not provide obligation lifecycle management, meta-policy conflict resolution, dispensations that waive obligations in specific circumstances, and ontological reasoning over domain class hierarchies commonly found in applications such as healthcare, cybersecurity, or data privacy. We propose AgenticRei, which realizes key governance requirements such as obligations, dispensations, policy conflict resolutions, and reasoning over policies, as well as the basic permit/prohibit constraints. We use a deontic policy language built on the Rei framework, expressed as OWL (Web Ontology Language) and evaluated at runtime by a high-performance logic engine entirely outside the LLM. The same pipeline governs both tool invocations by the agent and agent-to-agent messages. We show through examples that deontic policies capture governance constraints around security and privacy that mostly cannot be expressed in current production engines. Our approach composes naturally with industry-standard frameworks like A2AS.
items.why: Propuesta crucial de gobernanza para agentes que ejecutan acciones autónomas, superando el simple control de acceso.
items.title_es: Políticas deónticas para la gobernanza en tiempo real de sistemas de IA agentica
